agent1 (Round 1 summarized): Two anti-amyloid monoclonal antibodies—lecanemab and donanemab—are currently FDA-approved for treating early-stage Alzheimer’s disease, targeting amyloid-beta plaques and demonstrating clinically meaningful reduction in cognitive decline. These agents represent established therapies within this class, confirming their role in modern Alzheimer’s care. 

Final Answer:\boxed{A}

The provided information indicates that both lecanemab and donanemab are FDA-approved for use in patients with early-stage Alzheimer’s disease due to their ability to clear amyloid beta aggregates associated with neurodegeneration. The presence of multiple approved anti-amyloid therapeutics underscores progress toward targeted interventions based on pathological mechanisms underlying dementia progression.

Final Answer:\boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
